other_observers: Debbie Behnfield, Matt Clark, Susan Bonfiglio, Gwen Moore, Frank Ferrell, Lee & Linda Farrell, Scott Somershoe, Dan Stringer, Bill & Woody from Texas
description: This appears to be a worn Juvenile. Distinguishing this bird from the Common Terns I viewed a couple of weeks ago are rounder head, lighter carpal bar & wing pattern (white secondaries & white-tipped greater coverts, & translucent, thin black-tipped primaries.) There was just one Tern. It flew in, stayed around a couple of feet in from the shoreline and mostly preened for 20-30 minutes. It would fly away for a minute and return, then it left for 30 minutes or so and returned to the same approximate spot on the Chatfield marina Sandspit. On October 3, 2015 our group was able to approach the bird to within 50 feet and capture some okay images.
